items.title: Kering announces Gucci and L'Oreal 50-year exclusive beauty licence, one year ahead of schedule
items.summary: Kering confirmed Gucci and L'Oreal signed a 50-year exclusive beauty licence, accelerating the handover from the existing Coty deal by a year. Coty gets roughly $400M for early redemption; the new L'Oreal license becomes effective mid-2027. CEO Luca de Meo said the deal lets Gucci and L'Oreal begin shaping Gucci Beauty's next chapter earlier than planned.

items.title: ケリング、「グッチ ビューティ」のロレアル移管を1年前倒し 27年7月開始へ
items.summary: Japanese trade coverage of the Gucci-L'Oreal beauty licence acceleration explains the deal mechanics for the Japan trade audience, framing it against Coty's loss of the Gucci fragrance/beauty business and questioning what it signals about Kering's urgency to fix Gucci's underperforming beauty segment relative to its fashion revenue.

items.title: 구찌와 로레알, 50년 미용 라이선스 계약 체결
items.summary: Korean financial-news coverage of the Gucci/L'Oreal beauty license deal, relaying deal terms (roughly 6.3 trillion won Kering Beaute sale context, Coty redemption payment) for Korean retail-investor and industry audiences tracking Kering's post-Gucci-slump restructuring moves.

items.source: Sina Entertainment (新浪娱乐/嘉人 syndication)
items.title: 28岁95后男演员张凌赫登《嘉人》七月刊，3小时售罄14万本
items.summary: Chinese entertainment press reports Gucci brand ambassador Zhang Linghe's July Marie Claire China cover (his second major fashion-magazine cover in three months) sold out a 140,000-copy limited print run within three hours, with the online pre-sale hitting 5.72 million yuan in first five minutes. Coverage frames it as evidence of Gucci's China ambassador strategy driving measurable commercial buzz.

items.title: 张凌赫嘉人封面热议
items.summary: Weibo-sourced fan discussion around Zhang Linghe's Gucci-partnered Marie Claire cover shows a genuinely mixed reaction: alongside celebratory comments, some fans and onlookers pushed back on the hype as overblown ("张凌赫爆了个寂寞啊" - roughly "the hype fizzled"), with comparison threads to actor Yang Yang turning into fandom friction rather than unanimous praise.

items.source: CBNData (第一财经商业数据中心)
items.title: 一日连关两店，Gucci在中国"失宠"了吗？
items.summary: Chinese business-data outlet compiles Xiaohongshu sentiment on Gucci's continued China store closures, quoting viral comments like "you could travel for 20,000 yuan and keep the memories, but a Gucci bag just sits in your closet" and noting Gen Z increasingly views Gucci as design-stagnant ("设计越来越无聊") and only worth buying at discount or secondhand, where resale prices have also compressed sharply (a 1955 bag falling from 13,000 to 6,500 yuan cited).

items.title: GUCCI的12小时直播秀，是不是在作秀？
items.summary: Chinese marketing-industry community outlet critically dissects a recent Gucci 12-hour livestream event, questioning whether it was substantive brand-building or empty spectacle ("作秀" = putting on a show for show's sake), reflecting skepticism among China's marketing/creative professional community toward Gucci's recent always-on content strategy.

items.title: 루이비통도 다 뺀다…시내면세점 떠나는 명품
items.summary: Korean business daily reports Gucci has withdrawn from Shilla Duty Free Seoul and Lotte Duty Free World Tower branches as luxury brands abandon downtown Seoul duty-free in favor of department stores and airport locations, part of Kering's broader plan to close roughly 125 Gucci stores globally by 2030, with Asia cited as roughly 40% of the closures.

items.title: グッチが新グローバル・ブランドアンバサダーにaespa ニンニンを起用
items.summary: Japanese fashion trade outlet confirms Gucci named aespa member Ningning as new global brand ambassador, noting her prior appearances at the Gucci Primavera Milan show; kept in the pool as background context for ongoing K-pop-ambassador coverage still circulating in Japan/Korea entertainment press this week, though the announcement itself is dated.

items.title: 开云集团在新老板的重振计划下着眼于利润率增长一倍
items.summary: Chinese financial press covers Kering's turnaround plan under CEO Luca de Meo targeting doubled operating margins by 2030, including the store-network reset (~100 net closures planned in 2026, roughly 40% in Asia including Korea/Japan/China) and Demna's product-architecture reset toward leather craftsmanship over logo-driven items — cited here as continuing background for Gucci's China retrenchment still discussed in NEA financial media this month.
